sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.77 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$k$be a positive integer such that there is an insoluble finite group with exactly$k$conjugacy classes. Is it true that a finite group of maximal order with exactly$k$conjugacy classes is insoluble? { R.\,Heffernan, D.\,MacHale} No, it is not; see Table 1 in (A.\,Vera-Lopez, J.\,Sangroniz, , { 280}, no.\,5--6 (2007), 676--694).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
